answer:
To calculate \(\Delta H^\circ_{77°F}\) for the reaction \(\text{HCl}(g) \rightarrow \text{H}^+(aq) + \text{Cl}^-(aq)\), use the formula:

\[
\Delta H^\circ_{\text{rxn}} = \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{products}) - \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{reactants})
\]

Substituting the given values:
\[
\Delta H^\circ_{\text{rxn}} = [0 + (-158.8)] - [-87.54] = -158.8 + 87.54 = -71.26 \text{ Btu}
\]

The correct answer is \(\boxed{H}\).
